steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: On the left: “A recent study” is a noun phrase serving as the sentence subject. On the right: “why: [explanation]” follows the blank. The blank must contain the main verb of an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
The main verb of an independent clause must be a finite verb—a conjugated form showing tense and subject agreement. “Explains” is the only finite verb among the choices and correctly serves as the main predicate.

The other three are non-finite forms (two participles and an infinitive) that cannot function as a clause's main verb and would create a sentence fragment.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
